title: ryania parviflora
intro: Ryania parviflora, also known as ryania, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Ryaniaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, due to the presence of ryanodine, a toxic compound that has been isolated from its roots. The ryania plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including pain relief, reducing fever, and treating various skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis. In traditional medicine, ryania is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and may be used in combination with other herbs to create a range of herbal preparations, including teas, salves, and topical creams.
